Evaluating moves: left changes little and fills gaps without immediate merges; up would pull lower tiles upward and likely break structure in lower columns; down moves small tiles away from the top but leaves the strong top row intact and can set up lower merges later; right is dangerous because it shifts the top row and moves the 32 out of the corner. Best sub-goal is maintaining the cornered highest tile and stable ordering, so choose down.

Candidate actions: Up would pull tiles upward and break the strong bottom-oriented setup. Down keeps high tiles anchored but causes little improvement and can separate future merges. Left compresses rows safely: row2 becomes [2,4,0,0], row3 stays [64,16,32,0], row4 stays [2,4,16,4], preserving bottom structure and creating empty space on the right without risking the 64. Best sub-goal now is maintain a stable corner/edge structure and maximize future merge flexibility. Choose left.

experiences.intentions: Key state: 4x4 2048 board with 3 empty spaces. Rows are [2,0,0,0], [4,4,4,2], [16,16,4,16], [2,4,128,2]. Highest tile 128 is anchored near bottom-right but not yet in a stable corner chain. Goal: maximize merges while preserving space and ideally keep the 128 on an edge/corner. Candidate actions: Left gives immediate merges in row2 (4+4->8) and row3 (16+16->32), creating space and moving tiles toward a compact structure; Right also merges row2 and row3 but shifts the 128 into the interior/left less favorably relative to corner stability; Up gives only one merge in col4 and drags smaller tiles upward, reducing structure; Down gives one merge in col2 and may disturb future organization. Best sub-goal now is to gain two merges and open cells without moving the 128 away from edge support, so choose left.

experiences.intentions: Key state: the 128 tile is at row 2, col 3 with 64 below it and a rightmost column of [4,4,4,2]. There are 5 empty spaces, so survival is fine; priority is consolidating while preserving/strengthening the high-tile corner structure. Goal: keep largest tiles grouped on the right and create merges without scattering. Candidate actions: Up would merge the top two 4s in the rightmost column into 8 and stack [16,128,64] in the third column with 128 near the top, generally good for maintaining a strong right side. Down would merge the lower 4s in the rightmost column but move 128 away from the top-right and place 64 above it, less stable. Left would drag the 128 away from the right edge, weakening structure. Right keeps the 128 on the right and merges the rightmost column similarly, but it also shifts the top row to [0,8,16,4] unchanged and bottom row to [0,0,8,2] unchanged; overall fewer beneficial consolidations than Up. Best action: up.

experiences.intentions: Key state: 4x4 2048 board with only 3 empty spaces. Bottom-right row ends with [2,2], and top row has a single 2 with empties around it. Highest tile 128 is at row 2 col 2, with strong descending structure in column 2/left side. Goal: preserve space while creating merges and avoiding disruptive shifts that break the board. Candidate actions: Left merges the bottom-row [2,2] into 4 and compacts rows, but top row only becomes [2,0,0,0]. Right also merges [2,2] but shifts the 128 away from the left-side structure. Up creates a top-row merge of the two 2s in column 3 and keeps high tiles anchored, but does not open as much space. Down merges the two 2s in the last column and tends to keep larger tiles lower, but may disturb the current arrangement. Best sub-goal is increase empty cells while maintaining the monotonic high-value cluster; Left does this with an immediate merge and good compression without heavily scattering large tiles.
experiences.tasks: Achieve the highest possible tile in 2048 by merging tiles strategically.
